name: Pinehurst Pension Trust
description: Pinehurst Pension Trust is one of Connecticut's largest public pension funds, managing retirement assets for over 38,000 state employees and teachers. The fund maintains a diversified portfolio across public equities, fixed income, and an expanding alternatives program targeting 20% allocation. Known for its rigorous manager selection process and preference for established track records over five years.

name: Larkspur Endowment
description: Larkspur Endowment manages the long-term investment assets of a prominent New England research university. The endowment follows a Yale-model approach, with heavy exposure to private equity, venture capital, and real assets. The investment team of 12 professionals maintains a 25-year return target of CPI+5% with a preference for top-decile alternative managers.

name: Mendoza Family Office
description: Mendoza Family Office oversees the investment assets of the Mendoza family, founders of a prominent Latin American real estate and infrastructure conglomerate. The office pursues an opportunistic strategy across private equity, hedge funds, and direct investments. They prefer co-investment rights and exclusive deal access, and recently hired a former Goldman Sachs partner as CIO.

name: Northwind Capital Partners
description: Northwind Capital Partners is a New York-based fund of funds manager with a focus on alternative investments across hedge funds, private equity, and real assets. Managing capital for institutional clients including endowments, foundations, and sovereign wealth funds, Northwind is known for its deep due diligence process and long-standing manager relationships built over 25 years.

name: Glenfield Insurance Group
description: Glenfield Insurance Group manages general account and surplus assets for one of the Northeast's largest property and casualty insurers. The investment team has recently expanded its alternatives allocation from 8% to 14%, focusing on private credit, infrastructure, and select hedge fund strategies with low volatility profiles. Regulatory capital constraints drive a preference for liquid and semi-liquid vehicles.

name: Cobalt Bridge Foundation
description: Cobalt Bridge Foundation is a San Francisco-based philanthropic foundation focused on climate resilience, education equity, and global health initiatives. The foundation's investment office manages the endowment with a 5% spending policy and a growing focus on impact-aligned alternatives, particularly climate tech venture and ESG-screened private credit managers.

name: Aldercrest Capital
description: Aldercrest Capital is a fundamental long/short equity manager focused on North American industrials, technology, and consumer sectors. Founded by former Tiger Management analysts, the fund has compounded at 18.4% net since inception with a Sharpe ratio of 1.2. The team recently closed a $400M raise from a Midwestern pension and has selectively expanded its investor base through warm introductions.

name: Vesper Macro Partners
description: Vesper Macro Partners is a discretionary global macro fund trading rates, currencies, and commodities across developed and emerging markets. Led by a former head of macro at Brevan Howard, the fund has generated uncorrelated returns since launch with a focus on tail-risk management. They recently launched a new emerging markets sleeve focused on EM rates and FX.

name: Quartzlight Ventures
description: Quartzlight Ventures is a sector-agnostic early-stage venture fund with a focus on B2B software, climate tech, and developer tools. Managing their third fund of $450M closed in Q3 2025, the team has notable early investments in three unicorns and a robust LP base of university endowments and family offices. They are actively using the iConnections platform to expand access to institutional allocators.

name: Brightwater Credit
description: Brightwater Credit is a direct lending platform focused on middle-market companies in the US and Canada. Their oversubscribed third fund closed at $2.2B against a $1.8B target, reflecting strong demand from insurance companies and public pensions seeking yield with downside protection. The team maintains a gross default rate well below the industry average through conservative underwriting.

name: Stonemoor Real Estate Partners
description: Stonemoor Real Estate Partners is a private equity real estate firm focused on value-add and opportunistic strategies across Sun Belt markets. With $11B AUM across seven closed-end funds and a growing open-end core-plus vehicle, Stonemoor is one of the most active buyers of industrial, multifamily, and mixed-use assets in Texas, Florida, and Arizona.

name: Halcyon Quantitative
description: Halcyon Quantitative is a systematic multi-strategy hedge fund employing machine-learning models across equities, fixed income, and commodities globally. The flagship fund manages $6.2B and has delivered consistent alpha through multiple market regimes. Their alternative data team of 14 researchers is considered one of the most sophisticated in the industry.
